# Private Repository & BOM Override

This scenario shows how to incorporate a private, third-party repository that provides patched or extended-support versions of dependencies. It specifically demonstrates how a third-party Bill of Materials (BOM) can be used to override a publicly available, EOL (End-of-Life) dependency with a patched version from the private repository.

This is a common enterprise pattern when a vendor provides long-term support for a library (like Spring Boot) after its official EOL date.

#### Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    subgraph Public Repos
        SpringBoot["org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter:2.5.14 (EOL)"]
    end
    subgraph Private Repo
        PatchedStarter["org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter:2.5.14.ACME"]
        VendorBOM["com.thirdparty.springboot:patched-bom:1.0.0"]
        VendorBOM -. manages .-> PatchedStarter
    end
    Root[Project Root] -- imports --> VendorBOM
    Root --> SpringBoot_Declared("spring-boot-starter (version from BOM)")

```

#### SBT (build.sbt)
The `build.sbt` file first adds the private repository to the list of `resolvers`. Then, it imports the vendor's BOM. This typically requires a plugin like `sbt-bom` to provide the `mavenBomImport` setting. The actual dependency is declared without a version, letting the imported BOM control which version is used.

```{.scala include="/demos/sbt-demo/scenario-16-private-bom/build.sbt" snippet="repo-and-deps"}
```

#### Expected Intuition
"I want to use Spring Boot, but I need the version that my vendor supports. By adding their repository and importing their BOM, my build should automatically pick up the patched versions they provide."

#### Actual Resolution Results
Resolves `org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter:2.5.14.ACME`.
    *   *Reason*: The project explicitly adds the private repository as a resolver, making its artifacts available. The imported `mavenBomImport` constrains the versions of dependencies in the graph, and it dictates that `spring-boot-starter` should be version `2.5.14.ACME`.

#### Classpath Reality
The classpath contains `spring-boot-starter-2.5.14.ACME.jar`, not the public EOL version.

#### Key Takeaway
Adding a repository makes its artifacts *available*, but it is the combination of the repository and a version control mechanism (like an imported BOM) that allows you to safely override public dependencies with private, patched versions.
